name: cachetools
what_it_does: Extensible memoizing collections and decorators for Python — provides thread-safe LRU, LFU, TTL, and RR cache implementations with memoize decorators. cachetools features: LRUCache (Least Recently Used), LFUCache (Least Frequently Used), TTLCache (time-to-live expiration), RRCache (Random Replacement), MRUCache (Most Recently Used), TLRUCache (Timer-based LRU), cached()/cachedmethod() decorators, custom key functions, maxsize enforcement, cache.getsizeof() support for sized values, and thread-safe operations with optional lock parameter. Pure Python, zero dependencies.
use_cases: Agent LRU memoization — from cachetools import LRUCache, cached; cache = LRUCache(maxsize=128); @cached(cache); def fetch_user(user_id): return db.get(user_id) — LRU memoize; agent caches user lookups; maxsize=128 keeps 128 most recently used; older entries evicted automatically; thread-safe with lock=threading.RLock()

Agent TTL cache — from cachetools import TTLCache, cached; cache = TTLCache(maxsize=100, ttl=300); @cached(cache); def get_config(key): return config_service.get(key) — time-to-live; agent caches config for 300 seconds; entries expire automatically; fetch fresh on expiry; maxsize limits memory

Agent method caching — from cachetools import cachedmethod, LRUCache; class DataService: def __init__(self): self.cache = LRUCache(maxsize=50); @cachedmethod(lambda self: self.cache); def expensive_query(self, key): return self.db.query(key) — instance method cache; agent caches instance method results; each instance has its own cache

Agent custom key function — from cachetools import cached, LRUCache; def ignore_second_arg(a, b): return a; cache = LRUCache(maxsize=100); @cached(cache, key=ignore_second_arg); def fetch(url, timeout): return requests.get(url, timeout=timeout) — custom key; agent ignores non-essential arguments in cache key; same URL cached regardless of timeout value

Agent sized cache — from cachetools import LRUCache; def content_size(value): return len(value); cache = LRUCache(maxsize=1024*1024, getsizeof=content_size); cache['key'] = large_content — size-aware cache; agent limits cache by total bytes not entry count; getsizeof callback returns size of each value; maxsize is total size budget
not_for: Persistent/disk caching — cachetools is in-memory only; for disk persistence use cachelib FileSystemCache or diskcache
Distributed caching — cachetools is single-process; for distributed use Redis via cachelib/redis-py
Simple single-function caching — functools.lru_cache is simpler and faster for basic use cases
best_when: In-process caching with eviction policy control — cachetools provides LRU/LFU/TTL policies with clean decorator API, filling the gap between functools.lru_cache (basic) and Redis (distributed).
avoid_when: Disk persistence needed (use cachelib), distributed caching (use Redis), or simple single-function memoization (use functools.lru_cache).

pricing.notes: cachetools is MIT licensed. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. Performance: LRUCache get/set: O(1) — uses OrderedDict internally. TTLCache: O(log n) on expiry cleanup. All operations <1ms in-process. Thread safety adds lock overhead — use only when needed. Pure Python — slower than functools.lru_cache C extension for simple cases.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: KeyError for cache miss on direct access. ValueError for negative maxsize. Raises standard Python exceptions that are clear and expected. No custom exception hierarchy needed — simple get/set interface.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: cache[key] = value is idempotent. @cached decorated function is idempotent for same arguments (returns cached result). TTLCache entries expire deterministically based on time. Cache operations are deterministic for given state.
known_agent_gotchas: Thread safety requires explicit lock — cachetools.cached(cache) is NOT thread-safe by default; agent concurrent code: @cached(cache, lock=threading.RLock()); without lock, concurrent cache updates corrupt LRUCache OrderedDict; lock is optional for single-threaded use; always add lock for async or multi-threaded agents

@cached does not handle exceptions — if the wrapped function raises an exception, @cached does not cache the exception and does not retry; agent code: uncached exception leaves cache unchanged; next call re-executes function; if caching failed results is needed: implement manually with try/except in wrapped function

TTLCache expiry is checked on access not on schedule — TTLCache doesn't run background cleanup; expired entries only removed when accessed; cache.expire() forces cleanup manually; agent code checking cache size may see expired entries still counted in len(cache); call cache.expire() if accurate size matters

cachedmethod lambda must return the cache instance — @cachedmethod(lambda self: self.cache) requires the lambda to return a mutable mapping; if lambda returns None or wrong type, cachedmethod raises TypeError; agent code: ensure each instance initializes self.cache before first method call; __post_init__ for dataclasses

Cache key defaults include all arguments — @cached(cache) uses cachetools.keys.hashkey(*args, **kwargs) as default key; unhashable arguments (lists, dicts) raise TypeError on cache miss; agent code passing dicts to cached functions: use key=lambda *a, **kw: hashkey(str(a), str(kw)) or convert to tuple before calling

maxsize=0 means unlimited size — LRUCache(maxsize=0) has unlimited capacity (no eviction); LRUCache(maxsize=128) limits to 128 entries; agent code intending 'no cache' should use cachetools.NullCache not maxsize=0; unlimited cache will grow without bound for agents with many unique keys

security_score_components.security_notes: Pure Python in-memory library with no dependencies and no network calls. Do not cache secrets in shared cache instances. TTLCache limits secret exposure time. Cache poisoning: validate cached values if keys are user-controlled. Thread lock prevents race conditions in concurrent agents.
